source_alignment: FinOps Foundation FinOps Framework (Inform / Optimize / Operate)
Azure Well-Architected Framework - Cost Optimization pillar
Azure Cost Management + Billing best practices
Microsoft FinOps toolkit - FinOps hubs and FOCUS (FinOps Open Cost and Usage Specification)

rules.title: Underutilized (right-sizing candidate) Virtual Machine
rules.description: VM has sustained average CPU utilization below 5% and no memory pressure over a 14+ day lookback window, indicating it is oversized for its workload.
rules.remediation: Right-size to a smaller SKU in the same VM family using Azure Advisor / Azure Monitor metrics, or move the workload to Azure Container Apps / Functions if usage is bursty. Validate with a 30-day metrics baseline before resizing production workloads.
rules.estimated_savings: 20-50% of the VM's compute cost

rules.title: Idle Virtual Machine (candidate for deallocation)
rules.description: VM has near-zero CPU, network, and disk activity over a 30-day window, suggesting it is no longer in active use but is still incurring compute charges.
rules.detection_logic: avg_cpu_percent_30d < 1 AND network_bytes_30d < threshold AND power_state == 'running'
rules.remediation: Confirm with the resource owner, then stop/deallocate or delete the VM. If retained for DR/testing, schedule automated start/stop via Azure Automation or the Start/Stop VMs v2 solution.
rules.estimated_savings: Up to 100% of the VM's compute cost while stopped/deallocated

rules.title: Missing Reserved Instance / Savings Plan coverage on steady-state compute
rules.description: VM or scale set has run continuously (>90% uptime) for 30+ days on pay-as-you-go pricing with no matching Reserved Instance or Azure Savings Plan for Compute coverage.
rules.detection_logic: uptime_ratio_30d > 0.9 AND pricing_model == 'payg' AND no matching reservation/savings-plan SKU family
rules.remediation: Purchase a 1- or 3-year Azure Reservation for the specific VM size/region, or a broader Azure Savings Plan for Compute for flexible coverage across VM families. Use Azure Advisor reservation recommendations to size the commitment against actual usage.
rules.estimated_savings: Up to 65-72% versus pay-as-you-go compute rates

rules.title: Azure Hybrid Benefit not applied to eligible Windows Server / SQL Server workload
rules.description: Resource is running licensed Windows Server or SQL Server images and the organization has on-premises Software Assurance / subscription licenses available, but Azure Hybrid Benefit is not enabled on the resource.
rules.detection_logic: license_type != 'AHB' AND os_or_engine in ['WindowsServer','SQLServer'] AND eligible_licenses_available == true
rules.remediation: Enable Azure Hybrid Benefit on the VM/SQL resource (licenseType=Windows_Server or Windows_Server_Perpetual) to reuse existing on-prem licenses instead of paying the embedded license cost in the Azure meter.
rules.estimated_savings: Up to 40% on Windows VM cost; up to 55% on SQL Server cost

rules.title: Unattached Managed Disk
rules.description: Managed disk is not attached to any VM (diskState == 'Unattached') and has existed in that state beyond a 7-day grace period, continuing to accrue storage cost with no compute consuming it.
rules.remediation: Confirm the disk is not held for a pending restore/migration, then snapshot if retention is required and delete the disk, or downgrade to Standard HDD if it must be retained but is rarely accessed.
rules.estimated_savings: 100% of the disk's monthly storage cost if deleted

rules.title: Premium/high-performance disk tier on low-IOPS workload
rules.description: Disk is provisioned on Premium SSD or Ultra Disk tier but observed IOPS and throughput are consistently far below the tier's provisioned baseline, indicating over-provisioned performance.
rules.remediation: Right-size to Standard SSD or Standard HDD, or move to a smaller Premium SSD provisioned-size tier that still meets the observed IOPS/throughput ceiling.
rules.estimated_savings: 40-80% of the disk's monthly cost

rules.title: Orphaned / unassociated Public IP address
rules.description: A Standard SKU static Public IP address is not associated with any NIC, load balancer, application gateway, or NAT gateway, yet continues to incur an hourly charge.
rules.remediation: Release or delete the public IP if it is no longer required, or re-associate it with the intended resource.
rules.estimated_savings: 100% of the public IP hourly charge

rules.title: Idle / underutilized Load Balancer or Application Gateway
rules.description: Load balancer or App Gateway has zero or near-zero backend pool traffic over a 30-day window, or an Application Gateway is provisioned at a fixed high instance count with low request throughput.
rules.remediation: Delete unused load balancers/gateways, consolidate underused App Gateways, or enable autoscaling (v2 SKU) with a lower minimum instance count to match actual demand.
rules.estimated_savings: 20-60% of the resource's monthly cost

rules.title: Storage account without lifecycle management policy
rules.description: Blob storage account contains a significant volume of infrequently accessed data (cool/archive candidates) but has no Lifecycle Management policy configured to auto-tier or expire aging data.
rules.remediation: Configure a Lifecycle Management policy to transition blobs to Cool/Cold/Archive tiers based on last-access time, and set expiration rules for temporary or log data.
rules.estimated_savings: up to 50% (Cool), up to 80% (Archive) on tiered data

rules.title: Redundant or excessive storage replication tier
rules.description: Storage account is configured with GRS/RA-GRS/GZRS replication but the data is non-critical, reproducible, or already backed up elsewhere, making Locally Redundant Storage (LRS) sufficient.
rules.remediation: Downgrade to LRS or ZRS where cross-region redundancy is not a business requirement; confirm against data-residency and DR policies first.
rules.estimated_savings: ~50% of replicated storage cost

rules.title: Stale disk/VM snapshots retained indefinitely
rules.description: Snapshot has no expiration and has existed beyond the organization's backup retention policy (default checked: 90 days), accumulating storage cost.
rules.remediation: Apply a snapshot retention/expiration policy (e.g., via Azure Backup or Azure Policy), and delete snapshots beyond the required retention window.
rules.estimated_savings: 100% of snapshot storage cost upon deletion

rules.title: Non-production workload running outside business hours without auto-shutdown
rules.description: Resource tagged as dev/test/qa runs 24x7 with no auto-shutdown schedule, despite business hours usage patterns suggesting it is only needed during work hours.
rules.remediation: Enable VM auto-shutdown or the Start/Stop VMs v2 automation runbook to power resources down outside business hours; consider Dev/Test pricing subscription offers for eligible workloads.
rules.estimated_savings: ~65% (assuming 12x5 vs 24x7 runtime)

rules.title: App Service Plan over-provisioned relative to app load
rules.description: App Service Plan tier/instance count is sized well above sustained CPU/memory utilization and request volume of the apps hosted on it.
rules.remediation: Right-size the App Service Plan tier, consolidate low-traffic apps onto a shared plan, and enable autoscale rules instead of a static high instance count.
rules.estimated_savings: 20-40% of App Service Plan cost

rules.title: AKS cluster node pool without autoscaling enabled
rules.description: AKS node pool is configured with a fixed node count instead of the cluster autoscaler, leading to persistent over-provisioning during off-peak periods.
rules.remediation: Enable the AKS cluster autoscaler with sensible min/max bounds per node pool, and consider spot node pools for interruptible workloads and Azure Container Apps/serverless for bursty jobs.
rules.estimated_savings: 20-50% of node pool compute cost

rules.title: Azure SQL / Cosmos DB provisioned throughput far exceeds consumption
rules.description: Database is provisioned with fixed DTU/vCore or RU/s capacity that is consistently underutilized relative to actual consumption over a 14-30 day window.
rules.remediation: Move to serverless tier (Azure SQL Database serverless, Cosmos DB serverless/autoscale) or right-size the provisioned tier; enable Cosmos DB autoscale to eliminate manual RU/s guesswork.
rules.estimated_savings: 30-60% of database compute cost

rules.title: Missing cost allocation tags (governance / showback-chargeback gap)
rules.description: Resource is missing one or more mandatory cost-allocation tags (e.g., CostCenter, Owner, Environment, Application), preventing accurate showback/chargeback and cost attribution required by the FinOps 'Understand Usage and Cost' capability.
rules.remediation: Apply required tags directly or via Azure Policy 'Require a tag on resources' with remediation tasks; consider Azure Policy tag inheritance from resource group/subscription to reduce manual tagging burden.
rules.estimated_savings: Not directly cost-reducing; enables accurate allocation and downstream optimization

rules.title: No budget or cost alert configured at subscription/resource-group scope
rules.description: Scope has no Azure Cost Management budget with action-group alerts configured, reducing the ability to detect anomalous spend before it becomes a material overage.
rules.remediation: Create Cost Management budgets with forecast and actual thresholds (e.g., 50/80/100%) wired to an action group (email/Teams/webhook), and enable Cost Anomaly Detection alerts.
rules.estimated_savings: Not directly cost-reducing; reduces risk of unplanned overspend

rules.title: Azure Advisor cost recommendations outstanding and unactioned
rules.description: Azure Advisor has open cost recommendations (e.g., shutdown, right-size, reserved instance) older than 30 days that have not been actioned or dismissed with justification.
rules.remediation: Establish a recurring FinOps operating cadence (e.g., bi-weekly) to triage Advisor cost recommendations; action, snooze with a reason, or formally accept the cost with owner sign-off.
rules.estimated_savings: Varies by recommendation; typically surfaced directly in Advisor

rules.title: Development/Test subscription not using Dev/Test pricing offer
rules.description: Subscription hosts only non-production workloads (per tagging/naming convention) but is not enrolled in the Azure Dev/Test pricing offer, which provides discounted meter rates for eligible services.
rules.remediation: Migrate eligible dev/test workloads to a Dev/Test subscription (requires Visual Studio/MSDN or CSP Dev/Test eligibility) to access discounted VM, SQL, and other service rates.
rules.estimated_savings: Up to 40-80% depending on service

rules.title: ExpressRoute circuit or VPN Gateway provisioned above required bandwidth tier
rules.description: Circuit or gateway SKU/bandwidth tier is provisioned well above the sustained peak utilization observed over a 30-day window.
rules.remediation: Downgrade the circuit bandwidth tier or gateway SKU to match sustained peak demand plus reasonable headroom; review egress patterns before downsizing.
rules.estimated_savings: 10-30% of networking cost

rules.title: Duplicate or unused Azure Container Registry / Log Analytics workspace
rules.description: Resource shows no ingestion/pull activity over 30+ days, indicating it may be a leftover from decommissioned workloads or duplicated tooling.
rules.remediation: Confirm with the owning team, then decommission unused registries/workspaces or consolidate log ingestion into a shared workspace with appropriate retention/tiering.
rules.estimated_savings: 100% of the resource's ongoing cost if decommissioned

rules.title: Log Analytics workspace using Pay-As-You-Go tier without commitment tier evaluation
rules.description: Daily data ingestion volume is consistently high enough (>=100 GB/day sustained) to qualify for a Commitment Tier discount, but the workspace remains on Pay-As-You-Go pricing.
rules.remediation: Evaluate and switch to the appropriate Log Analytics Commitment Tier that matches sustained ingestion volume; also review verbose diagnostic categories and reduce retention on low-value tables.
rules.estimated_savings: Up to 30% of ingestion cost at qualifying volumes

rules.title: Unused or excess Azure Bastion / VPN Gateway standing infrastructure
rules.description: Azure Bastion host shows no session activity over a 30-day window while continuing to incur a fixed hourly charge.
rules.remediation: Deploy Bastion using the Developer SKU for occasional access, or deallocate/delete and re-provision on demand via IaC when access is actually needed.
rules.estimated_savings: Up to 100% of the Bastion hourly charge

rules.title: Multiple overlapping FinOps/cost tools or exports without a single source of truth
rules.description: More than one Cost Management export or third-party cost tool is configured against overlapping scopes without a documented single source of truth, risking inconsistent reporting.
rules.remediation: Consolidate to a documented primary reporting source (e.g., FOCUS-aligned Cost Management export) and treat other tools as downstream consumers, per the FinOps Framework 'Manage the FinOps Practice' capability.
rules.estimated_savings: Not directly cost-reducing; improves reporting integrity

rules.title: Autoscale not enabled on Virtual Machine Scale Set with variable load
rules.description: Scale set runs a fixed instance count despite exhibiting variable CPU/request load across the day/week, meaning peak capacity is paid for around the clock.
rules.remediation: Configure autoscale rules based on CPU, memory, or queue-length metrics with appropriate min/max/default instance counts and cooldown periods; consider mixing in Spot instances for stateless, interruptible capacity.
rules.estimated_savings: 20-45% of scale set compute cost

rules.title: No FOCUS-aligned cost data export configured for the scope
rules.description: The subscription/billing scope has no scheduled Cost Management export using the FOCUS (FinOps Open Cost and Usage Specification) dataset. FOCUS is the FinOps Foundation's open standard for cost and usage data; the Microsoft FinOps toolkit (FinOps hubs, Power BI reports) and most modern FinOps tooling build on it. Without a FOCUS export, cross-cloud/cross-tenant reporting, savings summaries, and year-over-year trend analysis require ad-hoc, inconsistent extracts.
rules.detection_logic: no Cost Management export with dataset type 'FocusCost' scheduled for the scope (az costmanagement export list / Microsoft.CostManagement/exports)
rules.remediation: Create a daily FOCUS cost export in Cost Management (Exports > Create > FOCUS cost dataset) to a Data Lake Storage Gen2 account, or deploy Microsoft FinOps hubs (aka.ms/finops/hubs) to manage exports, parquet conversion, and ingestion automatically. Requires Cost Management Contributor on the scope and the Microsoft.CostManagementExports resource provider registered.
rules.estimated_savings: Not directly cost-reducing; prerequisite for reliable savings measurement, commitment-discount tracking, and trend analytics

rules.title: Multi-subscription/multi-tenant estate without a scalable FinOps data platform
rules.description: The organization reports cost across many subscriptions, tenants, or clouds using only the Cost Management portal or manual exports. Per Microsoft FinOps toolkit guidance, organizations that outgrow native Cost Management capabilities should adopt a centralized, FOCUS-aligned data platform (FinOps hubs with Azure Data Explorer or Microsoft Fabric Real-Time Intelligence) rather than building a bespoke pipeline, gaining multi-tenant reporting, negotiated/commitment-discount savings summaries, and fast year-over-year analytics.
rules.detection_logic: scope spans multiple subscriptions/tenants AND no FinOps hub instance detected (no resource group containing the co-deployed hub set: Data Factory + Data Lake storage + Key Vault, optionally Data Explorer/Fabric RTI, typically tagged/named 'finops-hub')
rules.remediation: Deploy Microsoft FinOps hubs (aka.ms/finops/hubs/deploy): register the CostManagementExports and EventGrid resource providers, plan public/private networking with network admins, deploy the hub template (optionally with Data Explorer — starts at ~$120/mo + ~$10/mo per $1M monitored, or ~$5/$1M storage-only), then configure FOCUS exports for each scope. Deployment requires Contributor + RBAC Administrator (or Owner) on the target subscription. Query via KQL, the bundled Power BI reports, or Data Explorer dashboards.
rules.estimated_savings: Not directly cost-reducing; typically unlocks commitment-discount and waste findings at scale that portal-only reporting misses

rules.title: Log Analytics workspace without cost controls (retention / daily cap)
rules.description: Log Analytics workspace has interactive (analytics-tier) retention raised above the free 31/90-day window and/or no daily ingestion cap configured, so runaway or verbose log sources translate directly into unbounded cost. Log ingestion and retention are among the most common silent cost growers in Azure estates.
rules.remediation: Set an appropriate daily cap (workspaceCapping.dailyQuotaGb) as a circuit breaker on non-critical workspaces; reduce interactive retention (retentionInDays) and rely on long-term/archive retention per table for compliance data; move verbose, rarely-queried tables (e.g., ContainerLog, AppTraces, diagnostics) to the Basic or Auxiliary table plan; use Data Collection Rule (DCR) transformations to drop noisy columns/rows at ingestion; review top tables by ingestion volume with 'Usage | summarize IngestionVolume=sum(Quantity) by DataType | order by IngestionVolume desc'.
rules.estimated_savings: 15-60% of workspace ingestion/retention cost depending on table-plan and DCR-filter adoption
